tool.name: capture_evidence
tool.description:
Capture proof artifacts (HTTP requests, command output, screenshots) linked to vulnerability findings.
Use this tool whenever you discover a vulnerability to save the evidence that proves it exists. This creates a professional evidence trail for the penetration test report.

parameter.name: evidence_type
parameter.description: Type of evidence: "http" (request/response pair), "command" (terminal output), or "screenshot" (base64 PNG)

parameter.name: content
parameter.description: The evidence content — HTTP response body, command output, or base64-encoded screenshot

parameter.name: vuln_id
parameter.description: Vulnerability report ID to link this evidence to (e.g. "vuln-0001")

parameter.name: description
parameter.description: Human-readable description of what this evidence shows

parameter.name: url
parameter.description: URL for HTTP evidence or screenshot context

parameter.name: method
parameter.description: HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, etc.) — for HTTP evidence

parameter.name: status_code
parameter.description: HTTP response status code — for HTTP evidence

parameter.name: command
parameter.description: The command that was executed — for command evidence

parameter.name: exit_code
parameter.description: Command exit code — for command evidence

tool.examples:
# Capture HTTP evidence for an XSS finding
http <script>alert(1)</script> reflected in response vuln-0001 XSS payload reflected in search results page https://example.com/search?q=<script>alert(1)</script> GET 200
# Capture command output evidence
command uid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=0(root) vuln-0003 Command injection confirmed — executed 'id' command curl https://target.com/api?cmd=id 0
tool.notes:
ALWAYS capture evidence when: 1. A vulnerability is confirmed (link to vuln_id) 2. A tool output shows interesting findings 3. HTTP responses contain sensitive data or unexpected behavior
Evidence is saved to the run's evidence/ directory and indexed automatically.

tool.name: list_evidence
tool.description: List captured evidence artifacts, optionally filtered by vulnerability ID.

parameter.name: vuln_id
parameter.description: Filter by vulnerability ID (e.g. "vuln-0001"). If omitted, lists all evidence.

returns.description: Response with list of artifact summaries

tool.examples:
# List all evidence
# List evidence for a specific finding
vuln-0001
